lab.name: Nanoelectronics Metrology & Failure Analysis Lab
lab.location: ASU – MacroTechnology Works (MTW) / Engineering Research Center
lab.description: Interdisciplinary research group focused on nanoelectronics, nanoscale analytical imaging, and correlative metrology for emerging devices and 2D materials.

tools.name: Qnami ProteusQ Scanning NV Microscope
researchArea: Magnetic materials at the atomic scale
tools.shortDescription: Complete scanning NV (nitrogen-vacancy) quantum microscope for imaging surface magnetic fields of advanced materials at the nanoscale.
tools.detailedDescription: The Qnami ProteusQ is a turnkey quantum microscope based on scanning NV magnetometry (SNVM). It combines NV center magnetic field sensing with scanning probe microscopy to simultaneously capture sample topography and local magnetic fields with nanoscale resolution. The system is designed for room-temperature operation and comes with dedicated control and analysis software, making quantitative magnetic imaging accessible without requiring deep NV physics expertise.

specs.measurementTechnique: Scanning NV magnetometry (SNVM) using NV centers in diamond
keyFeatures: Single NV center sensitivity for atomic-layer magnetic signals
Qualitative and quantitative magnetic imaging modes
Uses Qnami Quantilever probes optimized for high optical throughput
Turnkey alignment-free confocal optics and integrated electronics
specs.magneticSensitivity: Better than ~2 µT/√Hz in continuous-wave ODMR mode (typical system value)
typicalApplications: Magnetic textures in thin films and multilayers
controlSoftware: LabQ software for SNVM and ODMR

tools.safetyNotes: Follow standard laser safety practices when working near the optical beam path. Observe ESD precautions when loading sensitive nanoelectronic or spintronic samples. Avoid mechanical shocks to the Quantilever probes and maintain a clean, dust-free environment around the optics and scanner.

tools.name: Park Systems NX-Hivac High Vacuum AFM
tools.shortDescription: High-vacuum atomic force microscope designed for failure analysis and air-sensitive materials, enabling high-accuracy nanoscale measurements with minimal tip and sample damage.
tools.detailedDescription: The Park NX-Hivac is a research-grade AFM engineered for high-vacuum operation, providing a clean and stable environment for nanoscale characterization. By operating under high vacuum rather than ambient or dry nitrogen, it improves measurement accuracy and repeatability while reducing probe and sample wear. The system combines Park's orthogonal scan architecture and True Non-Contact™ mode with automated vacuum control, making it well suited for semiconductor device failure analysis and studies of air-sensitive or reactive materials.

specs.environment: High vacuum chamber optimized for low outgassing
specs.vacuumLevel: Down to ~10^-5 Torr (typical), reached within a few minutes
specs.scanSystem: Orthogonal XY scan with decoupled Z for low cross-talk
operationModes: Advanced modes such as SSRM and SCM when integrated with external electronics
keyFeatures: High vacuum measurement for better accuracy and repeatability
Reduced tip and sample damage compared to ambient measurements
Automated vacuum control with dedicated Hivac Manager
Supports advanced failure analysis workflows in semiconductor devices
typicalApplications: Dopant profiling via Scanning Spreading Resistance Microscopy (SSRM)
Carrier distribution via Scanning Capacitance Microscopy (SCM)
Surface morphology of air-sensitive thin films and 2D materials

tools.safetyNotes: Ensure all samples and holders are compatible with high vacuum and free of volatile contaminants before loading. Follow vacuum system procedures when venting or pumping down the chamber. Avoid overtightening sample clamps and be cautious when approaching the surface to protect both the AFM tip and delicate devices.
